reality_check: Real-world saving: 8–13%. ARAI lab cycle does not reflect stop-go urban traffic. Independent test by NDTV Auto 2023 found 9.4% saving in city conditions.
No lifecycle data published. Self-declared marketing tagline with zero third-party audit. Toyota India has not submitted Fortuner LCA to any public registry.
Tailpipe-only metric. ~5.4t manufacturing CO₂ unreported. EEA fleet average 2023 for B-segment = 5.1–6.3t mfg CO₂. Supply chain emissions ignored.
MG Hector uses 2.0L petrol engine. ~9.2 L/100km real world per AutoCarIndia test. No lifecycle data. Badge engineering — clean claim on a high-emission vehicle.
8.0 L/100km petrol ARAI certified. No hybrid option at India launch. Zero LCA data provided to EEA or any registry. Contradicted by own emissions data.
Tailpipe zero, but 12.0t manufacturing CO₂ + 4.2t battery disposal. Total lifecycle = 25–35t depending on grid. EV breakeven vs Prius: ~3 years at KA grid.
60.5 kWh LFP battery = 8.47t disposal CO₂. Manufacturing = 15.1t. In China grid (0.61 kg/kWh) the EV advantage over ICE takes 6–8 years to materialise.

claim_text: Electrified MPV For a Better Tomorrow
reality_check: Self-hybrid claim valid but incomplete. No full LCA published for India market. Toyota Japan LCA does not account for India's 0.82 kg/kWh average grid.
Honda publishes tailpipe data only for India variant. Full lifecycle including steel stamping and paint shop emissions not disclosed for Indian manufacturing plant.
